text: Hyperliquid's fee revenue reached approximately $1.9 million in 24 hours, with 100% of fees used to buy back and burn 49,360 $HYPE tokens, totaling around 42.9 million tokens burned valued at $1.52 billion. The buyback mechanism is dynamic, with more aggressive repurchases when $HYPE price is lower, supporting token value and reducing supply.
author: elfa_2039873538040164721

text: A coordinated market manipulation scheme occurred on Hyperliquid involving the $XPL token, where 7 accounts deposited $1.85 million and used leveraged longs to pump the price, withdrawing $4.63 million in collateral and profiting nearly $3 million. This artificial price inflation caused liquidations and losses to the Hyperliquid liquidity pool. The attack was specific to $XPL due to thin spot liquidity and buyers on other exchanges. This event highlights risks in leveraged trading and the importance of monitoring for manipulation.
author: elfa_2040073330439471191

text: Hyperliquid continues to dominate the perpetual DEX market with a 64% market share, expanding into commodities and equity perps. The HIP-3 upgrade has driven quarterly volume to $133 billion, accounting for over 30% of Hyperliquid's total volume. The platform has implemented portfolio margin for accounts over $10k, improving capital efficiency and increasing trading size and revenue, which leads to more $HYPE token burns. Hyperliquid's ecosystem is rapidly growing with many builders launching projects and third-party frontends capturing significant trading volume. The platform also offers up to 5x leverage on $HYPE perps and has launched a mobile app in public testing. Additionally, $RIVER token is now live on Hyperliquid with a rewards campaign.

text: INTEL: Summary of USAF incidents today 1. F-15E fighter jet shot down; One pilot rescued, search for WSO ongoing. 2. USAF A-10 Warthog crashed in the Persian Gulf, pilot rescued. text: Most of the time, things that happen are our own fault. I don't plan to turn this channel into a philosophical channel any time soon, but I thought it would be an interesting insight to share this story. As many of you know I'm from London, born and bred, British; and with such background it shouldn't come as a suprise, I drive a 2006 Range Rover. The other day, I was parked in a single lane road, a narrow road with parking on both sides, and I was on the right hand side. I was perfectly legally parked within the parking bounds outlined by the white lines on the ground. Yet, within 5 minutes of parking there, a truck passed, and knocked my window. Thankfully, my mirror is a rotatable one, so all that happened was that the mirror got rotated a bit, and I just twisted it back. Dynamic. All good. The driver of the truck was apologetic, I saw there was no damage done, I just let him go. Then, more and more cars start to pass, and I notice that they're all just-about squeezing pass the cars in the single lane, and driving very slowly. I think nothing of it. "I'm parked legally, fuck em", I think to myself. 10 minutes later, a massive truck passes, completely smashes my mirror, and drives off without stopping. This is known as a "Hit and Run" in the U.K., I remember the first half of his number plate, I was parked outside a government building with a multitude of cameras, I could've easily made a police report. I could've made a claim against his insurance, I could've reported him to the police. I chose not to. Why? What's the emotional expenditure that it takes to call the police? What's the emotional expenditure to make an insurance claim against him? Larger than what I would like to expend. Why? Because the cost to replace a window is about $50. So the judgment which I made was, is my emotional expenditure < my financial gain. The answer was, no. So who's at fault here? Of course, it's me. I should've moved my car as soon as it was hit the first time. Legalities do not define conclusions. A person who is unable to think at the second-order/third-order would say that they were parked legally, make a police report, put a permanent mark against the truck driver on his insurance record for $50. A lot of these principles can be applied to crypto, don't blame the world for something that happened, most of the time, outcomes are your responsibility, even if they aren't your fault. I now drive with a spring and a piece of tape holding up my mirror.
author: admin#2386871401

text: QCP Weekly Wrap, 30 March–3 April 2026 Macro kept crypto in check, but resilience stood out. BTC held near $67k despite rising geopolitical tension and oil volatility, while ETH stayed firm and alts lagged. Positioning cleaned up, selling pressure eased, and short-dated hedging remained active, pointing to a less stretched market. The takeaway: support is there, but it is narrow. BTC is bid, not broadly chased. Subscribe to our newsletter for the full breakdown, including: • OTC desk positioning insights • Flow and volatility trends • Key macro catalysts to watch • Outlook for BTC, ETH, and broader risk assets
